info.name: Data for Change
info.description:
Myanmar open-data platform — REST API collection.

Built at a hackathon in May 2019 by Team Novit.

**Setup:** Start the backend with `docker compose up -d` from the backend repo, then import this collection alongside the `data-for-change.postman_environment.json` environment file.

**Base URL:** http://localhost:8100

**All API endpoints are public** — no authentication or token required.

**Important notes:**
- All data in the database uses `year=2019`
- The `getAllForOverall` response always shows `Standard of Living` as the top-level category name due to a loop bug in the original code — the variable data inside the response is correct
- `getAllForCompare` only returns religion/demographic data; other categories are commented out in the source
- City and year filtering in `getAllForCompare` is not implemented (WHERE clause is commented out)

See `docs/limitations.md` for full details.

item.name: 1. Health Check
item.description: Quick sanity checks to confirm the backend is running.

request.description: Root URL. Returns HTTP 302 → /login. Confirms Apache and Laravel are responding.
Returns HTTP 200 with the AdminLTE login page HTML. Confirms session/view system is working.

item.name: 2. Reference Data
item.description:
Read-only endpoints that return the taxonomy and geography. Call these first to get the UUIDs needed for the data endpoints.

All IDs are stable UUIDs — they do not change between runs (they come from the SQL dump).

request.description:
Returns all 6 categories, each with their subcategories and variables nested as `children` arrays.

This is the primary reference for variable IDs used in the data POST endpoints.

**Response shape:**
```json
{
  "data": [
    {
      "id": "uuid",
      "name": "Agriculture",
      "children": [
        {
          "id": "uuid",
          "name": "Live Stock",
          "children": [
            { "id": "uuid", "name": "Beef Production (ton)" },
            ...
          ]
        }
      ]
    },
    ...
  ]
}
```

**Categories returned:**
- Agriculture (Live Stock — 5 variables)
- Demographic (Religion — 5 variables)
- Health (Number of Hospitals — 7 variables)
- Natural Disasters and Hazards (Risk Percentage — 5 variables)
- Religious and Historical Sites (Heritage Buildings — 1 variable)
- Standard of Living (Drinking Water — 6 variables)

Same as 'Get all categories' but returns all names in Myanmar script (Unicode).

Example category names returned:
- ကျန်းမာရေး (Health)
- လူထုအချက်အလက် (Demographic)
- စိုက်ပျိုးရေးနှင့် မွေးမြူရေး (Agriculture)

Returns all 18 Myanmar regions/states, each with their cities nested as `children` arrays.

This is the primary reference for city IDs used in the data POST endpoints.

**Response shape:**
```json
{
  "data": [
    {
      "id": "uuid",
      "name": "Ayeyarwady",
      "children": [
        { "id": "uuid", "name": "Bogale" },
        { "id": "uuid", "name": "Danubyu" },
        ...
      ]
    },
    ...
  ]
}
```

**18 regions:** Ayeyarwady, Bago (East), Bago (West), Chin, Kachin, Kayah, Karen, Magway, Mandalay, Mon, Naypyidaw, Rakhine, Sagaing, Shan (East), Shan (North), Shan (South), Tanintharyi, Yangon

**356 cities total** across all regions.

item.name: Get all regions with cities (Myanmar script)

request.description: Same as 'Get all regions with cities' but returns names in Myanmar script.

query.description: When 'true', returns only region name and ID — no cities nested inside

item.name: 3. Statistical Data — Overview
item.description:
POST endpoint that returns filtered statistical data by variable(s), city(ies), and year.

**Required:** `year` must be `2019` — that is the only year stored in the database.

**Known bug in original code:** The `category.name` field in the response always shows `Standard of Living` regardless of which category the variable belongs to. The variable data inside `subCategory[].variable_city` is correct.

See `docs/limitations.md` for details.

item.name: Overview — Demographic: Multiple religion variables, two cities

request.description:
Returns Buddhist, Muslim, and Animist population for two Ayeyarwady cities (Bogale and Danubyu) in 2019.

Demonstrates multi-variable and multi-city filtering.

Returns number of Sangha (Buddhist monk) hospitals in Bogale city for 2019.

Variable: `Number of Sangha Hospitals` from the Health category.
Returns counts for Sangha hospitals and Mental hospitals across two cities.

Returns beef and pork production tonnage for Bogale city in 2019.

Variable IDs from Agriculture > Live Stock subcategory.

item.name: Overview — Standard of Living: Tap water

request.description:
Returns number of households using tap/piped water in Bogale city for 2019.

Variable from Standard of Living > Total Households on Drinking Water.

Returns flooding risk percentage for Bogale city in 2019.

Variable from Natural Disasters and Hazards > Risk Percentage by Township Area.

Returns heritage building count for Bogale city in 2019.

Variable from Religious and Historical Sites > Heritage Buildings.

Same as 'Buddhist population' but with `lang=mm`. Returns all name fields in Myanmar script.

Demonstrates the bilingual support of the API.

item.name: 4. Statistical Data — Compare
item.description:
POST endpoint that returns comparative data across regions and cities.

**Known limitation:** Only returns religion/demographic data. The disaster data section is commented out in the source. City and year filtering is also not implemented — all cities and all years are always returned regardless of the request body.

See `docs/limitations.md` for details.

request.description:
Returns religious demographic breakdown (Buddhist, Christian, Hindu, Muslim, Animist) for all cities grouped by region.

**Note:** The `cities` and `year` filter parameters are ignored — the endpoint always returns all data. This is a known limitation in the original code (WHERE clause is commented out).

**Response shape:**
```json
{
  "<region-uuid>": [
    {
      "region": { "id": "uuid", "name": "Shan (North)" },
      "city_name": "Pangsang",
      "Animist": 0,
      "Buddhist": 0,
      "Christian": 0,
      "Hindu": 0,
      "Muslim": 0
    },
    ...
  ],
  ...
}
```

18 region keys, 330 city entries total.
Same as 'Compare — Religion' but with `lang=mm`. Region and city names are returned in Myanmar script.

item.name: 5. Admin Panel (Web — Session Auth)
item.description:
Web routes for the admin panel. These are HTML-based (not JSON API) and require session authentication.

Authentication flow:
1. GET /login → get CSRF token from `<meta name="csrf-token">` in the HTML response
2. POST /login with `_token`, `email`, `password` → sets session cookie
3. Subsequent requests send the session cookie

**Demo credentials:** admin@data4change.com / password

Note: Import buttons in the admin panel will fail with file-not-found errors — the CSV source files are not in the repository. The panel is shown for demonstration only.

exec: // Extract CSRF token from the HTML response
console.warn('CSRF token not found in response');

request.description:
Loads the login page and extracts the CSRF token into the `csrf_token` environment variable via the test script.

**Run this before 'POST Login'.** The CSRF token is required for the login form submission.

exec: // After successful login, Laravel redirects to /import
console.warn('Login may have failed — status:', pm.response.code);

urlencoded.description: CSRF token — auto-filled by 'Get login page' test script

request.description:
Submits the login form. Requires the `csrf_token` variable to be set by running 'Get login page' first.

A successful login returns HTTP 302 → /import and sets a session cookie.

**In Postman:** Enable 'Automatically follow redirects' and 'Send cookies' to see the import panel HTML on the redirect.

**Demo credentials:**
- Email: admin@data4change.com
- Password: password

Returns the data import admin panel HTML (HTTP 200) if a valid session cookie is present.

Returns HTTP 302 → /login if no session is active.

**Run 'POST Login' before this request** to establish a session.

The panel shows import buttons for: Regions, Cities, Townships, Hospitals, Drinking Water, Religion, Live Stock, Disasters, Heritage Buildings.

item.name: 6. Auth-Protected API (Not Implemented)
item.description:
The API has a token-protected `/api/user` endpoint and a `/api/v1/` group defined in routes, but neither is implemented.

- `GET /api/user` redirects to login (HTTP 302) — no token-based auth is wired up
- `GET/POST /api/v1/*` — route group exists but is empty

These requests are included for documentation completeness. They will not return JSON data.

header.description: Token auth is not wired up — this header has no effect

request.description:
**NOT IMPLEMENTED.** Returns HTTP 302 → /login.

This endpoint uses `auth:api` middleware which expects a Laravel API token. No token-based authentication was implemented in the original codebase. The `api_token` column does not exist in the users table.
